text: When setting project goals, it helps to follow the SMART framework. This means goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying “improve the website,” a SMART goal might be “increase page loading speed by 20% within three months.” Break large goals into smaller milestones, such as completing a design review or testing a prototype. Each milestone should have clear deliverables, like a report or a working version of a feature. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved. This avoids misunderstandings and keeps the team working toward the same results.
